Pricing

Our prices reflect the competitive value of new and unworn watches, complete with all accessories and a valid manufacturer’s warranty. While we do accept offers to close deals, please understand that our prices are already highly competitive in the market.

Inspection

Before shipping, every watch undergoes a thorough inspection to ensure it matches the description in the listing. We check the timekeeping, case, bracelet, and overall condition, ensuring it’s in perfect order. Each watch is securely packed in its box to ensure safe delivery.

Returns

Buyers have two weeks to inspect their purchase. If the watch is not as described or does not function properly, you may return it for a full refund. Returns will only be accepted if the watch is in the same condition as it was shipped, including all original paperwork and accessories. Watches that have been worn, used, or altered in any way will not be eligible for a return.

Taxes

Sales tax is calculated and added at checkout based on your shipping address, in accordance with state and local laws. Chrono24 collects and remits these taxes directly, and we do not have the ability to waive or alter this amount.

Shipping & Delivery

• United States: All packages are shipped via Overnight shipping and can be delivered to a business address, FedEX office (Hold For Pickup) or a residential address.

• International: We welcome international buyers, and duties/customs fees are the buyer’s responsibility. Certain countries may have shipping restrictions due to insurance limitations, so please contact us to confirm availability.

Why Choose Us?

• Over 24 years of expertise and countless satisfied customers worldwide.
• A commitment to authenticity, transparency, and customer satisfaction.
• Competitive pricing, a 2-year warranty, and secure worldwide shipping.
• Chrono24’s escrow system ensures your funds are secure until your watch is delivered and confirmed.

We look forward to making your next watch purchase seamless and enjoyable. If you have any questions or need assistance, feel free to reach out for a consultation

Available now is a stunning, statement piece by Audemars Piguet, the Royal Oak 15400ST. This 41mm has fantastic wrist presence and wears more like a 43mm. Everything beautifully made and intricately perfected by AP starting with the octagonal shaped bezel, which has polished outer edges and a satin finished top with white gold bolts arranged in a captivating pattern. The stunning rhodium or metallic grey dial features a "Grande Tapisserie" pattern and looks absolutely stunning with white gold hands, indices, and the "AP" at 12oclock. The caseback on the underside displays a sapphire windowed display of the Calibre 3120 movement.

Specs:
• Reference: 15400ST.OO.1220ST.04
• Model: Royal Oak
• Case Size: 41mm

What’s Included:
• Outer AP box and inner AP green lacquered presentation box
• Papers
• Bracelet is fully linked (fits approx 7.75in wrist)

Condition:
This watch is in very excellent preowned condition with little to no signs of wear throughout. There are no deep scratches, dents, chips, etc. There are hardly any visible surface hairlines. The crystal is clean. If you look very carefully you might find a couple of hairlines that are very hard to notice mainly on the polished portions of the bezel/case and clasp. Overall it is in amazing condition. It is running and functioning flawlessly.

Photos are of the actual watch, taken with a professional lens — please excuse any dust picked up by the camera.

Shipping and Guarantee:
We ship fully insured via FedEx or UPS.
All watches are 100% authentic and exactly as described.
Questions? Reach out anytime — happy to assist!
Thanks for looking!

We ensure that every watch purchase is a seamless experience, backed by our commitment to authenticity and customer satisfaction.

All purchases come with our 2 year warranty and authenticity guarantee. We 100% guarantee the authenticity of every watch we sell. Every piece goes through our meticulous 7 step authentication process, and is also checked by our watchmaker to ensure there are no internal issues. Our warranty covers everything the original manufacturer's warranty would cover.

Rolex watches are accompanied by their original factory warranty card, box & manuals. White Tags for Rolex may or may not be included when pictured. Please contact us to confirm, if the White Tag is necessary for you.

All other timepieces come with the original manufacturer's warranty, box, papers, and more. With over 24 years of expertise in Watches & Jewelry, we guarantee the authenticity of every product we sell or offer a full refund. Our reputation is built on transparency and trust, with countless satisfied customers worldwide and an extensive reference list dating back to 2000. You can always give us a call to receive a friendly consultation to assist you in finding the perfect timepiece.

Our policies are designed to provide clarity and convenience for our customers.
Sales tax is applied based on your shipping destination.
International orders are welcome. Duties and customs fees are the responsibility of the buyer. All watches are in unworn condition and complete, unless otherwise stated.

Regarding pricing, our listed prices are competitive and reflect the value of new/unworn watches with full accessories and a valid manufacturer's warranty. However, we still do accept offers in order to close deals. We guarantee the authenticity and originality of all our items, backed by our registered dealer status and Chrono24's authenticity guarantee.

When placing an order, please note that while most items are in stock and ready to ship, some may be temporarily out of stock despite being listed as available. We strive to secure our next shipment promptly to fulfill your order.

For shipping, we exclusively use FedEx Priority Overnight within the United States, with flat-rate shipping options available for US and Canadian orders. We use the fastest possible shipping for overseas shipments. Please note that there are certain countries we are unable to ship to, so please inquire for details.

To ensure secure delivery, all need to be delivered to a local FedEX office for pickup, or a business address.

In conclusion, our company is committed to providing exceptional service and quality timepieces to our customers worldwide. We look forward to assisting you in your next watch purchase and invite you to reach out to us for any inquiries or assistance you may need.

Overview

A bold and meticulously finished example of Audemars Piguet’s legendary design language, this Royal Oak Offshore Diver combines iconic octagonal architecture with modern dive capability and AP’s unmistakable Méga Tapisserie dial. Recently serviced by Audemars Piguet in 2023 and fitted with a boutique-purchased OEM AP leather strap and quick-change conversion (approx. $2,900 upgrade), this piece offers both elevated character and turnkey reliability. One of the lifestyle photographs shows the watch resting atop the Audemars Piguet 150th Anniversary Book, a subtle nod to the heritage that continues to inspire the Offshore line.

Condition

Case: Excellent – Sharp brushing, crisp facets, and minimal handling marks.
Bezel: Excellent – Clean edges but some notable wear on the brushed surface upon close inspection. Minimal surface scratches from normal wear on polished edges as pictured.
Crystal: Excellent – Free of scratches or chips.
Dial & Hands: Excellent – Deep black Méga Tapisserie texture, clean printing, strong lume.
Bracelet/Strap: Excellent – OEM AP brown leather strap with contrasting stitching and boutique-installed quick-change system; light, even wear.
Movement: Excellent – Serviced by Audemars Piguet in 2023 (confirmed by AP Boutique). This piece was previously authenticated through Chrono 24.

Set & Accessories

Includes:

Audemars Piguet service box.

AP pictorial warranty/information card

OEM AP leather strap with AP stainless steel buckle

Boutique-installed AP quick-change strap conversion

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Rose Gold Black Dial Mens Watch 26320OR. Automatic self-winding chronograph movement. 18k rose gold octagonal case, 41 mm in diameter. 18k rose gold screw down crown and two pushers. Solid case back. 18k rose gold bezel punctuated with 8 signature screws. Scratch-resistant sapphire crystal. Black dial with Grande Tapisserie waffle pattern, luminous baton hour markers, and hands. Central Seconds hand. Three chronograph sub-dials -- running small seconds, 30 minutes, and 12 hours. 18k rose gold bracelet.

**NEW** The Royal Oak Extra-Thin Platinum / Green / 50th Anniversary, reference 16202PT.OO.1240PT.01, is a testament to Audemars Piguet's enduring legacy of innovation and elegance. **Absolutely New Unworn**

EXCLUSIVITY.
This watch embodies exclusivity as it is not only a special edition but also classified as a limited edition. Such designations enhance its desirability among collectors and enthusiasts alike.

Recognition.
The watch has gained prestigious recognition by being prominently featured on the wrists of numerous high- profile celebrities.

Unique Components.
The featured timepieces are distinguished by their patented quick-set date mechanism, showcasing impressive engineering and luxury craftsmanship.

Movement.
At the heart of this masterpiece lies the mechanical Caliber 7121, a marvel of precision engineering. With a frequency of 4.0 Hz and a power reserve of 52 hours, it ensures reliable performance. The movement, measuring 29.6 mm in diameter and 3.2 mm in thickness, comprises 268 parts and 33 jewels, showcasing the intricate artistry synonymous with Audemars Piguet.

Case.
Crafted from 950 platinum, the case of the Royal Oak Extra-Thin measures 39.0 mm in diameter and 8.1 mm in thickness. This luxurious material not only enhances the watch's aesthetic appeal but also offers a water resistance of up to 50 meters, ensuring durability and elegance in equal measure.

Dial.
The smoked green dial, adorned with a sunray pattern, is a visual delight. It features white gold applied hour markers and Royal Oak hands with luminescent coating, ensuring optimal readability. This harmonious blend of colors and textures reflects the watch's sophisticated character and timeless allure.

Strap.
The strap, also crafted from 950 platinum, complements the case's elegance. It is secured with an AP folding clasp, providing both comfort and security. This seamless integration of design elements underscores the watch's luxurious essence.

Complications.
The Royal Oak Extra-Thin Platinum / Green / 50th Anniversary offers essential functions, including hours, minutes, and date. These complications are elegantly integrated into the design, enhancing the watch's functionality without compromising its aesthetic appeal.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Chronograph 26240ST – 50th Anniversary Edition
The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Chronograph Ref. 26240ST “50th Anniversary” is a standout celebration of five decades of the iconic Royal Oak design. This 41 mm stainless steel chronograph blends Audemars Piguet’s signature octagonal bezel and integrated bracelet with modern updates and commemorative touches that honor the Royal Oak legacy. ￼

At its heart is the in‑house Calibre 4401 automatic chronograph movement—a fully integrated flyback chronograph with column wheel control and approx. 70‑hour power reserve—offering exceptional precision, performance, and mechanical beauty visible through the sapphire exhibition caseback with a special “50 Years” winding rotor. ￼

The dial features the signature “Grande Tapisserie” pattern—available in several striking colors like khaki green, blue, black or silver—paired with applied markers and a date display between 4 and 5 o’clock for balanced, sporty elegance. ￼

A refined blend of heritage and innovation, the 26240ST 50th Anniversary is both a horological milestone and a versatile luxury sports watch that stands out in any collection. ￼

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore Chronograph, reference 25721ST.OO.1000ST.07, is a bold, high-performance sports watch that combines rugged design with luxury craftsmanship. Its 42 mm stainless steel case features the iconic octagonal bezel with eight screws and a robust, integrated bracelet — designed for durability and comfort. The silver dial, with its “Méga Tapisserie” pattern and contrasting black subdials, offers excellent legibility and a dynamic, modern aesthetic. Its oversized case, bold design, and sporty character make it a standout in AP’s lineup — perfect for those who appreciate high-impact style, Swiss engineering, and the rebellious spirit of the Offshore collection.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Chronograph “Panda” – 26331ST – 2023

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Chronograph “Panda” Ref. 26331ST stands as one of the most striking and sought-after modern interpretations of the iconic Royal Oak design. Combining the unmistakable Gérald Genta-inspired case with a clean and highly legible dial, this reference has become a favorite among collectors who appreciate both heritage and bold aesthetics.

Crafted in stainless steel, the 41 mm case features the signature octagonal bezel secured by eight hexagonal screws, finished with the impeccable blend of satin brushing and polished bevels that define Audemars Piguet’s craftsmanship. The integrated bracelet flows seamlessly into the case, offering both comfort and a refined wrist presence that is instantly recognizable.

The standout feature of this piece is its highly coveted “Panda” dial configuration. The crisp silver-toned “Grande Tapisserie” dial is contrasted by black subdials, creating a sporty yet elegant look that enhances readability while giving the watch its nickname. The chronograph layout is perfectly balanced, complemented by white gold applied hour markers and Royal Oak hands with luminescent coating.

Powering the watch is the self-winding caliber 2385, a reliable and precise automatic chronograph movement known for its slim profile and smooth operation. Visible through the sapphire caseback, the movement reflects Audemars Piguet’s commitment to both performance and finishing.

Discontinued and increasingly difficult to source, the 26331ST “Panda” represents a compelling opportunity for collectors seeking a modern Royal Oak with strong long-term appeal. Its versatility allows it to transition effortlessly from casual wear to more formal settings, making it one of the most wearable chronographs in the luxury space.

Key Features:

Highly sought-after “Panda” dial with black subdials

Iconic Royal Oak design by Gérald Genta

Stainless steel 41 mm case with octagonal bezel

“Grande Tapisserie” dial with exceptional finishing

Self-winding chronograph movement (Caliber 2385)

Discontinued reference with strong collector demand

Nicknamed “Combat”, this Offshore blends rugged military inspiration with the bold architecture the collection is known for. Built for collectors who want presence with attitude.

Presents itself in mint condition with little to no signs of wear. Showcases a champagne dial that introduces a warm, textured surface that shifts subtly under light, balanced by darker subdials that add depth and contrast. Features a green ceramic bezel that frames the dial with a matte, tactical finish, reinforcing the watch’s utilitarian edge. Camouflage accents across the strap and case details bring a layered, rugged character.

Functions include hours, minutes, seconds, date, and chronograph. Screw-down crown allows precise time setting and quick date adjustment. The pushers operate the chronograph function with the top pusher starting and stopping timing, while the bottom pusher resets it. Subdials track elapsed seconds, minutes, and hours, while luminous hands and markers ensure strong visibility in low-light conditions.

Comes as a full set, double-boxed, including the watch pillow, warranty card, and warranty card holder, booklet or manual and quick user guide.

Camouflage executions within the Offshore line introduce a more aggressive, field-inspired identity to an already bold design. Stands out through contrast, texture, and unmistakable wrist presence — made for collectors who prefer strength with character.

A cool Audemars Piguet for everyday wear, this ladies’ Royal Oak from the 1990s balances a slightly rugged presence with elevated two-tone details. The stainless steel case is paired with a yellow-gold screwed bezel, framing a steel-grey tapisserie dial accented by raised luminous hour markers and matching hands, with a date aperture at 3 o’clock. Powered by a quartz movement for easy, low-key maintenance, the watch is fitted with a glossy black AP crocodile strap that appears seamlessly integrated into the case and is secured by a steel deployant clasp with gold-tone AP fill. A neo-vintage Royal Oak seen in a variety of configurations, this two-tone example stands out as a versatile everyday favorite.

The Royal Oak Perpetual Calendar ref. 26574ST brings high complication into stainless steel, balancing the density of information with clarity. Its blue Grande Tapisserie dial organizes day, date, month, leap year, the photorealistic moonphase, and a rare 52-week indicator across a highly legible layout, with white gold markers providing crisp contrast. Despite its intricate perpetual calendar display, it maintains a slender 9.5mm profile. The steel case and bracelet keep the presentation restrained, directing attention to the dial. Within the case is the ultra-thin caliber 5134, driving the full calendar cycle with long-term precision.

This pre-owned Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Blue Dial Perpetual Calendar Steel Watch 26574st is accompanied by papers and the manufacturer's box.

DESCRIPTION
The Limited Edition Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore Ref. 26471SR.OO.D101CR.01, also known as the “Bucherer”, is a rare and highly sought-after release—one of only 300 pieces ever produced. This striking Offshore delivers a bold fusion of rose gold luxury and deep blue sport aesthetics. Its rich blue “Méga Tapisserie” dial—arguably one of AP’s best color executions—pairs flawlessly with rose gold accents throughout, creating unmatched wrist presence. The blue crocodile hornback strap with contrast orange stitching ties the entire color palette together, giving the watch dynamic visual depth. Featuring a rose gold bezel, unique AP deployant clasp, and an exhibition caseback showcasing the intricacies of its movement, this limited Offshore blends sporty refinement with everyday versatility. A perfect expression of high-end craftsmanship and bold design.

Key Features and Specifications
• Model: Royal Oak Offshore Chronograph – Limited Edition “Bucherer”
• Reference: 26471SR.OO.D101CR.01
• Limited Edition: 1 of 300 Pieces Worldwide
• Case Size: 42mm
• Case Material: Stainless Steel Case with 18-Carat Rose Gold Accents
• Bezel: 18-Carat Rose Gold Bezel with Signature Hex Screws
• Case Thickness: Approx. 14.4mm
• Dial: Deep blue “Méga Tapisserie” dial with rose gold Arabic numerals and accents — a top-tier AP dial execution
• Movement: Audemars Piguet Caliber 3126/3840 (Automatic Chronograph)
• Number of Jewels: 59
• Power Reserve: Approximately 50 hours
• Functions: Hours, minutes, small seconds, date, chronograph
• Crystal: Scratch Resistant, Anti-Reflective Sapphire
• Strap: Blue crocodile hornback strap with striking orange contrast stitching
• Clasp: Unique AP deployant clasp
• Water Resistance: 100 meters / 330 feet
• Box/Papers: includes original box, Archived Papers from Audemars Piguet

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Selfwinding 15450ST (2021) – Grey Dial

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Selfwinding 15450ST is a refined interpretation of one of the most iconic luxury sports watches ever created. Originally designed by Gérald Genta in 1972, the Royal Oak redefined haute horology with its bold octagonal bezel, visible screws, and integrated bracelet — and the 15450ST carries that legacy forward with modern precision and timeless sophistication.

This 2021 example features a stunning grey “Grande Tapisserie” dial, offering depth, texture, and understated elegance. The cool grey tone shifts subtly in different lighting, giving the watch a dynamic presence while maintaining a versatile aesthetic suitable for both formal and everyday wear.

Crafted in stainless steel with the unmistakable Royal Oak integrated bracelet, the 37mm case size delivers perfect proportions for collectors who appreciate a slightly more refined wrist presence without sacrificing impact. Inside beats the reliable and beautifully finished automatic Caliber 3120, visible through the sapphire caseback — a movement celebrated for its robustness and craftsmanship.

The 15450ST is increasingly sought after, as discontinued references in optimal sizes continue to gain attention from serious collectors.

Bold Presence with Unmistakable Offshore Attitude
The Royal Oak Offshore Chronograph “Batman” is built for collectors who want scale, strength, and instant recognition. Where the classic Royal Oak is about restraint, the Offshore leans into power. The black and blue colorway sharpens that identity, delivering contrast and depth without drifting into gimmick territory.

Why Collectors Love It
The Offshore appeals to buyers who already understand the Royal Oak lineage and want something louder, stronger, and more expressive. This black and blue colorway is especially desirable because it balances aggression with sophistication. The blue accents give it personality without overwhelming the design, making it easy to wear with elevated casual looks or modern luxury streetwear. It is chosen by collectors who are comfortable with visibility and enjoy a watch that carries presence before a word is spoken. The Offshore does not whisper status. It delivers it with confidence.

The Audemars Piguet 26470ST, known to enthusiasts as the "Elephant," represents the pinnacle of the Royal Oak Offshore’s evolution. Since the collection's debut, AP has used the Offshore as a platform for material innovation and bold horological statements. This 2014 model is defined by its slate gray "Méga Tapisserie" dial and contrasting deep black chronograph pushers and screw-locked crown. Integrated with a durable gray rubber strap, the "Elephant" is one of the most socially recognized luxury sports watches in existence. It provides an unmistakable wrist presence that commands respect in any professional or social setting, cementing its status as a modern horological icon.

A video of this AP can be viewed under title "Audemars Piguet 1994 Royal Oak Military Dial Steel 14800ST".

A sportier and more rugged take on the popular Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Ref. 14800, this steel model dated to 1994 features an attractive black dial with luminescent Arabic numerals and plots, paired with matching hands—often regarded as a “military dial.” The sweep seconds hand carries a luminescent arrow tip, while the date aperture at 3 o’clock completes the layout. The crown is signed by Audemars Piguet, and the watch is powered by a caliber 2225 automatic movement.
Fitted with an AP brown leather strap that has developed a pleasing patina and contrasting white stitching, the watch is secured by an AP-signed steel deployant clasp. This reference reflects the mid-1990s shift toward more utilitarian, tool-leaning Royal Oak variations. Rugged yet attractive readily comes to mind for this variant.

This Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore 26730TI is an unworn example, offered in factory condition and completely unpolished. Crafted from lightweight titanium, the watch features a bold black Méga Tapisserie dial with contrasting subdials, creating a strong, modern aesthetic consistent with the Offshore line’s sporty character. The signature octagonal bezel with exposed screws, brushed surfaces, and sharp edges remain crisp and untouched.

The 44 mm case houses the in-house Calibre 4401 automatic flyback chronograph movement, featuring column-wheel architecture, vertical clutch, and a 70-hour power reserve. Sapphire crystals protect the dial and exhibition caseback, offering durability and a clear view of the movement.

This watch is offered as a complete full set, including the original Audemars Piguet box, warranty card, booklets, and accessories. The original strap is unworn and secures comfortably on the wrist. The Jewel of Le Brassus: Royal Oak Mini Frosted Gold 67630BA

The Audemars Piguet 67630BA is more than a watch; it is a piece of kinetic jewelry. Bringing back the beloved "Mini" size of the late 90s, this 23mm masterpiece is rendered in 18-carat yellow gold and treated with the Florentine "Frosted Gold" finish.

This ancient hammering technique, championed by jewelry designer Carolina Bucci, indents the gold to create a surface that sparkles with the intensity of diamond dust. The monochromatic look is completed by a matching gold-toned "Petite Tapisserie" dial, offering a seamless and radiant aesthetic.


Powered by the Quartz Calibre 2730, designed specifically to fit the slender case while offering carefree maintenance, this timepiece bridges the gap between high horology and high jewelry.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Chronograph 26240ST – 50th Anniversary Edition
The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Chronograph Ref. 26240ST “50th Anniversary” is a standout celebration of five decades of the iconic Royal Oak design. This 41 mm stainless steel chronograph blends Audemars Piguet’s signature octagonal bezel and integrated bracelet with modern updates and commemorative touches that honor the Royal Oak legacy. ￼

At its heart is the in‑house Calibre 4401 automatic chronograph movement—a fully integrated flyback chronograph with column wheel control and approx. 70‑hour power reserve—offering exceptional precision, performance, and mechanical beauty visible through the sapphire exhibition caseback with a special “50 Years” winding rotor. ￼

The dial features the signature “Grande Tapisserie” pattern—available in several striking colors like khaki green, blue, black or silver—paired with applied markers and a date display between 4 and 5 o’clock for balanced, sporty elegance. ￼

A refined blend of heritage and innovation, the 26240ST 50th Anniversary is both a horological milestone and a versatile luxury sports watch that stands out in any collection. ￼

There are certain designs that feel less like watches and more like objectsexpressions of form and texture as much as timekeeping. The Audemars Piguet Cobra Ref. 5403 is one such creation. Emerging from a period when the maison was exploring sculptural, jewelry-driven wristwatches, the Cobra stands apart for its seamless integration of case and bracelet, its surface rendered in a fluid, scale-like pattern that wraps the wrist with a distinctly tactile presence. Executed here in white gold, the effect is at once substantial and soft, the intricate links catching light in a way that feels almost organic.

The dial elevates this example into rarer territory. Crafted from aventurine, it presents as a deep, midnight field subtly alive with fine, shimmering inclusionsan effect that recalls a clear night sky scattered with distant stars. Against this richly textured surface, diamond-set hour markers appear almost suspended, while slender hands glide quietly across the dial. The date aperture at three oclock is discreetly framed, maintaining the balance of the composition without disrupting its calm symmetry.

What distinguishes the Cobra is not simply its construction, but its coherence. The case dissolves into the bracelet with no clear boundary, creating a continuous form that feels considered from every angle. Worn on the wrist, it sits with a natural fluidity, the weight of white gold tempered by the articulation of its links.

Preserved in excellent condition, this example captures the essence of Audemars Piguets more expressive periodwhere traditional watchmaking met a freer, more sculptural approach to design. With its rare aventurine dial and beautifully integrated bracelet, it stands as a compelling reminder that some of the most enduring watches are those that blur the line between object and ornament.

A sportier and more rugged take on the popular Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Ref. 14800, this steel model dated to 1994 features an attractive black dial with luminescent Arabic numerals and plots, paired with matching hands—often regarded as a “military dial.” The sweep seconds hand carries a luminescent arrow tip, while the date aperture at 3 o’clock completes the layout. The crown is signed by Audemars Piguet, and the watch is powered by a caliber 2225 automatic movement.
Fitted with an AP brown leather strap that has developed a pleasing patina and contrasting white stitching, the watch is secured by an AP-signed steel deployant clasp. This reference reflects the mid-1990s shift toward more utilitarian, tool-leaning Royal Oak variations. Rugged yet attractive readily comes to mind for this variant.

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Tourbillon Chronograph ref. 25977OR.OO.D002CR.01 is a commanding expression of haute horlogerie, combining two of the most revered complications in a single 44 mm Royal Oak case. Launched in the early 2000s and produced in extremely limited numbers, this model features the hand‑wound calibre 2889, a column‑wheel chronograph paired with a one‑minute tourbillon, delivering both precision and visual drama. The 18K rose gold case, with its iconic octagonal bezel and Grande Tapisserie black dial, exudes the refined contrast AP collectors prize. Measuring 12.9 mm thick and spanning 58 mm lug‑to‑lug, the proportions offer both presence and wearability. The movement, visible through the sapphire display back, is meticulously finished with Côtes de Genève, anglage, and polished screw heads, underscoring AP’s artisanal pedigree. Water resistant to a modest 20 meters, this is a watch designed for connoisseurs who value technical mastery over rugged utility. Its rarity and the dual complication combination make it highly sought after among seasoned collectors. This is not merely a timekeeper; it is a statement of mechanical artistry and Royal Oak heritage. Excellent condition with Audemars Piguet box and papers dated 2010.

Description:
The 26408OR is an eye-catching ode to the Summer of 2017, when the watch was released. Audemars Piguet launched it as a limited run of 500 pieces to celebrate that year's summer – a season that is reflected all over the watch. The 44mm case is made from solid pink gold in the alternative Offshore style (bigger case, bigger pushers, bigger strap). It is fitted to a heavy duty grain-textured white rubber strap and matched with a pink gold clasp. The dial of the 26408OR is the same iconic Mega Tapisserie pattern seen on all Offshore models, matching the strap with its striking white color. It features several pops of contrasting color, with navy numbers along the chapter ring, a navy seconds track beneath the chapter ring, navy-outlined subdials, and red-tipped hands. There is a small seconds subdial at 12 o'clock, date window at 3 o'clock, hour chronograph subdial at 6 o'clock, and minute chronograph subdial at 9 o'clock. Powering the 26408OR is Audemars Piguet's in-house Caliber AP 3126/3840 automatic movement with 50 hours of power reserve.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Selfwinding 15450ST (2021) – Grey Dial

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Selfwinding 15450ST is a refined interpretation of one of the most iconic luxury sports watches ever created. Originally designed by Gérald Genta in 1972, the Royal Oak redefined haute horology with its bold octagonal bezel, visible screws, and integrated bracelet — and the 15450ST carries that legacy forward with modern precision and timeless sophistication.

This 2021 example features a stunning grey “Grande Tapisserie” dial, offering depth, texture, and understated elegance. The cool grey tone shifts subtly in different lighting, giving the watch a dynamic presence while maintaining a versatile aesthetic suitable for both formal and everyday wear.

Crafted in stainless steel with the unmistakable Royal Oak integrated bracelet, the 37mm case size delivers perfect proportions for collectors who appreciate a slightly more refined wrist presence without sacrificing impact. Inside beats the reliable and beautifully finished automatic Caliber 3120, visible through the sapphire caseback — a movement celebrated for its robustness and craftsmanship.

The 15450ST is increasingly sought after, as discontinued references in optimal sizes continue to gain attention from serious collectors.

Key Features:

Model: Royal Oak Selfwinding

Reference: 15450ST.OO.1256ST.06

Year: 2021

Case Size: 37 mm

Case Material: Stainless Steel

Dial: Grey “Grande Tapisserie”

Movement: Automatic Caliber 3120

Bracelet: Integrated Stainless Steel Bracelet

Crystal: Sapphire

Water Resistance: 50 meters

Why This Watch Matters

The Royal Oak is more than a watch — it’s a cornerstone of modern luxury sports horology. The 37 mm 15450ST strikes a rare balance between wearability, collectibility, and investment potential. As discontinued Royal Oak references continue to tighten in supply, well-preserved examples from recent production years like 2021 represent a compelling acquisition for collectors seeking both heritage and long-term value.

At Investment Watches, we specialize in sourcing exceptional timepieces with enduring desirability — and this Royal Oak Selfwinding is a prime example.

[AUDEMARS PIGUET] [CODE 11.59 PERPETUAL CALENDAR] [41MM]

Reference: 26394OR.OO.D321CR.01
Condition: Excellent
Case Size: 41mm
Material: Red gold // Aventurine

Description:
The Code 11.59 perpetual calendar takes a sensible design and makes it striking. This model features the exact same 41mm proportions as any other Code 11.59, but it's made entirely from Audemars Piguet's proprietary red gold and features a perpetual calendar – one of the most complex movements in all of watchmaking. With this function, the dial displays months in a subdial at 12 o'clock, a leap year indicator within the same subdial, a date subdial at 3 o'clock, a moonphase indicator at 6 o'clock, a day indicator subdial at 9 o'clock, and a week indicator around the chapter ring. There is, quite simply, nothing else you could need on a dial. The cherry on top of this stunning watch is an aventurine dial that mimics a starry sky and catches the light at every possible angle. This Code 11.59 also features the updated Audemars Piguet deployment clasp. Powering the 26394OR is Audemars Piguet's in-house Caliber AP 5134 automatic movement with 40 hours of power reserve, visible through a sapphire crystal display caseback.

Featuring the iconic "Bleu Nuit, Nuage 50" Grande Tapisserie dial, this model boasts updated ergonomics, a 70-hour power reserve via Calibre 4302, and a special "50th" anniversary rotor.

Case & Design: 41mm stainless steel case with a 10.5mm thickness, featuring a glareproofed sapphire crystal, screw-locked crown, and 50m water resistance.

Dial: "Bleu Nuit, Nuage 50" (Night Blue, Cloud 50) dial with a "Grande Tapisserie" pattern, white gold applied hour-markers, and Royal Oak hands with luminous coating.

Movement: Automatic Calibre 4302, 32 jewels, operating at 28,800 vph (4Hz) with a 70-hour power reserve.

Bracelet: Stainless steel bracelet with an AP folding clasp.

Ergonomics: The 15510 series features subtle improvements over its predecessor (15500) for better wearability on the wrist.

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore Diver 15720ST.OO.A027CA.01 is a bold and modern take on one of the most iconic dive watches in the world. With its unmistakable Royal Oak design, this model combines luxury craftsmanship with genuine underwater performance.

Crafted from stainless steel, the 42mm case features the signature octagonal bezel with exposed screws, a hallmark of Audemars Piguet’s design heritage. The vibrant blue “Méga Tapisserie” dial gives the watch a sporty yet refined appearance, complemented by white gold hour markers and hands coated in luminescent material for excellent legibility in all lighting conditions.

At 10 o’clock, you’ll find the internal rotating bezel crown, allowing precise dive-time measurement, while the 3 o’clock crown ensures water resistance up to 300 meters (1,000 feet) — making this a true professional-grade diver.

Inside beats the Audemars Piguet Caliber 4308, a self-winding movement visible through the sapphire caseback. This movement provides a 60-hour power reserve and features a quick-set date and hacking seconds, blending precision and practicality.

The watch comes fitted on a blue rubber strap with a stainless steel pin buckle, offering both comfort and durability. It also includes AP’s quick-release strap system, allowing easy strap changes for different looks or occasions.

A perfect mix of sport and sophistication, the Royal Oak Offshore Diver 15720ST is ideal for those who want the bold AP presence with everyday versatility.

The watch is fitted with its original solid 18k rose gold Royal Oak bracelet, complete with a folding clasp for a secure and comfortable fit.

With 50 meters of water resistance, this Royal Oak Chronograph is suitable for daily wear while retaining its refined luxury character. This reference is highly sought after for its precious metal construction, classic blue dial, and timeless Royal Oak design.

Audemars Piguet is one of the top selling watch brands, mainly due to the Royal Oak. However, there are plenty of other fantastic offerings in their vintage catalog through the decades, especially with regards to their dress watches. AP are known for some of their highly technical and complicated dress pieces as well but for some those options might be too cumbersome to operate and maintain. For those who appreciate minimalist time only designs, there are plenty of interesting finds to discover in the brand today.
This particular ultra-thin ref. 5043 circa 1950s is housed in 18k yellow gold. It features a white dial with retailer signature from BVLGARI, making it an extremely rare find. The 33mm case is remarkably thin, made possible by the manual winding and legendary cal. 2003. This piece has remained in excellent vintage condition overall. There is a touch of aging at the dial's perimeter but still presents very nicely. The case has remained in sharp, unpolished condition with a crisp and markings. This piece comes accompanied by a blue gator strap and 18k AP signed AW pin buckle. For anyone looking for a high quality vintage dress watch that's truly special, this is a unique find we'd be hard pressed to source again.

Dial: There is a touch of aging on its perimeter but it is not distracting. No significant damage. The BVLGARI signature has been scrutinized for authenticity.

Bezel: Appears unpolished with light signs of use.

Hands: Are matching.

Case: Appears unpolished with very sharp edges, a crisp hallmark and markings still clearly visible. Correct unsigned crown.

Bracelet: 18k AW signed pin buckle and supple two piece gator strap in blue.

Crystal: Clear.

Accessories: NA

The movement is running well on our timegrapher and comes with our 6-month mechanical warranty.

A video of this AP is available to view online under title "Audemars Piguet Huitieme 18k Rose Gold 40mm Automatic 1980s".

An attractive piece from Audemars Piguet’s Huitième collection, this 1980s example shows that simplicity leaves a lasting impression. Crafted from 18k rose gold, the 40mm case offers a striking presence on the wrist — a modern size for the era, made more distinctive by the "Audemars Piguet" engraving along the bezel. The white dial is perfectly complemented by rose gold hands, including a sweep seconds hand, and balanced with raised silvered baton markers. A date aperture at 3 o’clock adds practicality, with the “AP” signature positioned off-center near the window. The outer minute track keeps the layout clean while enhancing legibility. Powering the watch is the Audemars Piguet caliber 2125 automatic movement, known for its slim profile — derived from the Jaeger-LeCoultre 889 base used by other renowned brands during the 1990s and 2000s. This watch is fitted with a burgundy AP crocodile strap and secured by an AP-signed 18k rose gold tang buckle, offering a handsome contrast to the rose gold case and adding a touch of precious metal. A versatile, everyday classic that exemplifies AP's less-is-more approach during the 1990s.

The spectacular Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore Diver in Lime Green Ref. 15710ST.OO.A038CA.01 has been added to our collection. This bold timepiece showcases AP’s mastery of avant-garde design with its electrifying lime green dial that transforms the iconic Royal Oak aesthetic into something truly extraordinary.

The commanding 42mm stainless steel case houses the signature “Méga Tapisserie” patterned dial, creating a mesmerizing texture that shifts dramatically under different lighting conditions. The vibrant lime green colorway is perfectly balanced with contrasting black accents on the inner rotating diving scale and subdial, offering exceptional legibility despite its expressive design.

Powered by AP’s in-house Calibre 4308 automatic movement visible through the exhibition caseback, this horological masterpiece delivers 60 hours of power reserve alongside genuine diving functionality with 300m water resistance.

Part of AP’s colorful Offshore Funky Diver collection, this particular variant represents one of the most sought-after contemporary offerings from Audemars Piguet—where limited production numbers and distinctive aesthetics have created exceptional demand among serious collectors.

A true statement piece that combines technical prowess with unapologetic boldness—perfect for the discerning enthusiast who values both exclusivity and mechanical excellence.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Chronograph – Ref. 26331ST.OO.1220ST.03

This listing is for an Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Chronograph, reference 26331ST.OO.1220ST.03. The watch features a 41mm stainless steel case with the iconic Royal Oak octagonal bezel secured by eight exposed hexagonal screws. The case and bracelet are finished with Audemars Piguet’s renowned combination of satin brushing and polished bevels, showcasing exceptional attention to detail.

The dial is a classic blue “Grande Tapisserie” pattern, fitted with applied white gold hour markers and Royal Oak hands, all with luminous coating for enhanced legibility. The chronograph configuration includes three sub-dials and a date display at 4:30, maintaining the balanced and instantly recognizable Royal Oak Chronograph layout.

Powering the watch is the Audemars Piguet Caliber 2385 automatic chronograph movement, visible through the sapphire crystal caseback. This movement is valued for its reliability, slim construction, and smooth chronograph operation. The watch is fitted with its original stainless steel Royal Oak bracelet, complete with a folding clasp that offers a secure and comfortable fit on the wrist.

With 50 meters of water resistance, this Royal Oak Chronograph is suitable for everyday wear while retaining its refined sports-luxury character. This reference is highly sought after for its stainless steel construction, blue dial, and timeless Royal Oak design.

A versatile and collectible chronograph from one of the most respected manufacturers in haute horology.

Audemars Piguet – Royal Oak – 15550ST – Blue Dial – 2023

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak 15550ST is a modern reinterpretation of one of the most iconic designs in horology. Released in 2023, this 37mm stainless steel masterpiece embodies the perfect balance between sportiness and elegance, remaining true to Gérald Genta’s legendary Royal Oak design while offering a refined, contemporary touch.

Its striking blue dial features the signature “Grande Tapisserie” pattern, creating depth and sophistication under shifting light. The white gold applied hour markers and Royal Oak hands, treated with luminescent coating, ensure exceptional legibility. Encased in brushed stainless steel with polished bevels, the octagonal bezel secured by eight white gold screws highlights the watch’s unmistakable character.

Powered by the selfwinding Calibre 5900, this reference delivers a power reserve of approximately 60 hours. A sapphire crystal caseback reveals the movement’s intricate finishing, underscoring Audemars Piguet’s dedication to craftsmanship. Completing the look, the integrated stainless steel bracelet with AP folding clasp offers unparalleled comfort and a seamless aesthetic.

This Royal Oak 15550ST is not only a symbol of timeless design but also a testament to modern Swiss watchmaking excellence. Whether worn casually or with formal attire, it stands as a definitive statement piece for collectors and enthusiasts alike.

As one of the most sought after variations of this specific Vampire AP model, the Royal Oak Offshore transcends conventional luxury timepieces, becoming instead an extension of the wearer's boldest inclinations. The self-winding manufacture caliber beats reliably beneath the sapphire caseback, visible like a mechanical heart powering this extraordinary creation.

For the discerning collector seeking a timepiece with both technical prowess and unmistakable presence, this "Vampire" edition represents one of the most coveted and distinctive iterations in the celebrated Royal Oak Offshore lineup.

Crafted entirely from 18-carat rosegold, the Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Extra-Thin ref. 15202OR is a modern icon that pays homage to the original 1972 Royal Oak “Jumbo.” Featuring a stunning white “Petite Tapisserie” dial and ultra-slim 8.1mm profile, this reference is the ultimate expression of minimalist elegance and horological sophistication.

The white dial is adorned with rosegold hour markers and hands, all filled with luminescent coating for perfect legibility. The signature AP logo is tastefully positioned at 12 o'clock, a subtle nod to the vintage inspiration behind this model. Powered by the in-house Caliber 2121, the movement is only 3.05mm thick and boasts a 40-hour power reserve—visible through the sapphire caseback.

Whether you're a seasoned collector or a first-time investor, the 15202OR stands out as one of the most desirable Royal Oak references ever created. Discontinued in 2022, its rarity and demand have only continued to rise, making this piece an essential asset in any high-value watch portfolio.

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak 15510ST.OO.1320ST.08 represents the latest evolution of one of the most iconic luxury sports watches ever made. Crafted entirely from stainless steel, this 41 mm model refines the classic Royal Oak design with subtle improvements, advanced mechanics, and a fresh, modern aesthetic.

The watch features a stunning blue “Grande Tapisserie” dial, a hallmark of the Royal Oak collection, radiating depth and sophistication under different lighting. The white gold applied hour markers and luminescent hands ensure exceptional readability while maintaining a clean, balanced design.

At its heart is the Audemars Piguet Caliber 4302, an in-house self-winding movement with a 70-hour power reserve, beating at 4 Hz for superior precision. The beautifully finished movement can be admired through the sapphire crystal caseback, showcasing its 22k gold rotor and fine hand-decorated details.

The newly refined integrated stainless steel bracelet offers improved comfort and fluidity on the wrist, complemented by the secure AP folding clasp. With 50 meters of water resistance, the 15510ST is ideal for daily wear while maintaining a luxurious, timeless appeal.

Elegant, powerful, and unmistakably AP — the Royal Oak 15510ST.OO.1320ST.08 is a perfect example of contemporary watchmaking excellence that honors the legacy of the original Royal Oak design.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore Chronograph “Navy”

26470ST.OO.A027CA.01

The captivating Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore “Navy” has arrived. Built for the open water and refined enough for an evening out—this is the ultimate timepiece for those who live without boundaries.

Evoking the timeless allure of deep ocean voyages, this exceptional timepiece presents the Royal Oak Offshore in a sophisticated navy blue configuration that captures the essence of nautical elegance. The striking blue “Méga Tapisserie” dial creates mesmerizing depth and texture, complemented by matching blue subdials and luminous white applied hour markers that ensure exceptional legibility in any condition.

The iconic 42mm stainless steel case commands immediate attention through its signature octagonal bezel with exposed titanium screws—a revolutionary design language introduced by Gerald Genta in 1993 that forever changed the landscape of luxury sports chronographs. The harmonious pairing of brushed and polished surfaces creates dynamic light play across every angle.

Powered by AP’s exceptional self-winding caliber 3126/3840 chronograph movement visible through the sapphire exhibition caseback, this masterpiece delivers 60 hours of power reserve alongside technical excellence that defines Audemars Piguet’s prestigious position in haute horlogerie. The column wheel chronograph mechanism ensures precise, responsive activation with every push.

The premium blue rubber strap with deployant clasp completes this nautical masterpiece—offering both sophisticated aesthetics and practical comfort that honors the Offshore’s adventurous spirit.

For the discerning collector seeking a timepiece that commands respect on any ocean or in any boardroom, this “Navy” edition represents the perfect fusion of technical prowess and maritime elegance.

The Code 11.59 is Audemars Piguet's most unique design since the introduction of the Royal Oak case shape back in 1972 – ironically, it is one their most traditional designs. The watch features a 44mm case crafted from solid 18k rose gold, paired with the iconic octagonal bezel secured by eight hexagonal screws. The case is finished with a combination of brushed and polished surfaces, highlighting Audemars Piguet’s exceptional craftsmanship.

The dial is a black “Méga Tapisserie” pattern, fitted with applied rose gold hour markers and Royal Oak hands, all with luminous coating for improved legibility. This movement is known for its durability, reliability, and smooth chronograph operation. The watch is fitted with a black rubber strap and an 18k rose gold Audemars Piguet pin buckle, providing both comfort and a secure fit.

One of the first!!
Per evaluation recently completed at Audemars Piguet in Le Brassus, Switzerland (and also at AP in Aspen) this iconic timepiece is one of the very first released in 18k yellow gold. This watch is one of the first series and is numbered in the mid-100's (with no alphanumeric reference). It is likely among the earliest examples of the 5402BA available for sale today with the 6 at the down dial.

This exquisite timepiece has been securely stored for over 25 years. It was recently sent to AP in Switzerland for service and restoration as part of the "Classic Parts" program designed specifically for the Royal Oak 5402 (estimated cost for this is approximately $6,000). It's an "all or nothing" program meaning that the owner of the watch may not refuse any part replacements by AP for "functional reasons OR if it will bring the watch closer to its original aesthetics." As such, ALL SERVICE WAS DECLINED in favor of preserving the integrity and originality of this coveted timepiece for buyer. All paperwork from Audemars Piguet to be provided with sale.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore "Panda" – 26170ST – 2014

The iconic Royal Oak Offshore “Panda” 26170ST is a standout timepiece from Audemars Piguet’s bold and performance-driven Offshore collection. This 2014 edition pairs a striking aesthetic with the brand’s renowned craftsmanship, making it a coveted model among collectors and enthusiasts alike.

Featuring a 42mm stainless steel case and the signature octagonal bezel with exposed screws, the watch commands attention with its crisp white Méga Tapisserie dial, contrasted by black subdials—earning it the beloved "Panda" nickname. The red chronograph hands add a sporty edge, while the black Arabic numerals and bold design elements reflect the Offshore’s rugged elegance.

Powered by the AP Caliber 3126/3840 automatic movement, the 26170ST offers a 50-hour power reserve and chronograph functionality with precision timekeeping. A black hornback alligator strap with a stainless steel pin buckle completes the piece, ensuring both comfort and durability.

Whether worn as a statement or held as an investment, this Royal Oak Offshore Panda is a modern classic that blends timeless appeal with contemporary sportiness.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak 15510ST – Black Dial – 2023

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak 15510ST represents the modern evolution of one of the most iconic luxury sports watches ever created. Designed by the legendary Gérald Genta and originally introduced in 1972, the Royal Oak continues to define contemporary haute horlogerie with its unmistakable octagonal bezel, integrated bracelet, and refined industrial aesthetic.

This 2023 reference 15510ST features the striking black “Grande Tapisserie” dial, offering a bold yet versatile presence on the wrist. The deep black texture enhances legibility while creating dynamic contrast against the white gold applied hour markers and Royal Oak hands, both filled with luminescent material for optimal readability.

Crafted in stainless steel, the 41 mm case maintains the signature brushed and polished finishing that has made the Royal Oak an icon. The seamless integration between the case and bracelet exemplifies Audemars Piguet’s exceptional craftsmanship, delivering both structural integrity and ergonomic comfort.

Powering this timepiece is the in-house Caliber 4302 automatic movement, offering a 70-hour power reserve and visible through the sapphire caseback. The movement’s meticulous finishing and 22k gold rotor reinforce the watch’s position as a benchmark in modern luxury watchmaking.

Whether worn as a daily statement piece or held as a long-term collectible, the Royal Oak 15510ST continues to be one of the most sought-after references in the secondary market.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak – Black Dial – 15510ST – 2022

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak 15510ST is the modern evolution of one of the most important designs in horological history. Presented in stainless steel with a sleek black Grande Tapisserie dial, this reference embodies the perfect balance between architectural sportiness and refined luxury—an unmistakable aesthetic that has defined the Royal Oak since 1972.

Released in 2022 as part of the model’s 50th-anniversary refresh, the 15510ST features upgraded finishing, enhanced movement performance, and subtle ergonomic improvements while preserving the bold geometric identity that made the Royal Oak an icon. Powered by the in-house Caliber 4302, celebrated for its accuracy, 70-hour power reserve, and beautifully decorated rotor, this watch delivers technical excellence behind its legendary silhouette.

The black dial version is among the most sought-after configurations, prized for its timeless versatility and strong wrist presence. With its integrated bracelet, hand-finished bevels, and signature octagonal bezel secured by white gold screws, the 15510ST remains one of the most recognizable luxury watches in the world.

Perfect for collectors seeking a contemporary Royal Oak with modern mechanics, or enthusiasts ready to add a true cornerstone piece to their collection.

One of the first!!
Per evaluation recently completed at Audemars Piguet in Le Brassus, Switzerland (and also at AP in Aspen) this iconic timepiece is one of the very first released in 18k yellow gold. This watch is one of the first series and is numbered in the mid-100's (with no alphanumeric reference). It is likely among the earliest examples of the 5402BA available for sale today with the 6 at the down dial.

This exquisite timepiece has been securely stored for over 25 years. It was recently sent to AP in Switzerland for service and restoration as part of the "Classic Parts" program designed specifically for the Royal Oak 5402 (estimated cost for this is approximately $6,000). It's an "all or nothing" program meaning that the owner of the watch may not refuse any part replacements by AP for "functional reasons OR if it will bring the watch closer to its original aesthetics." As such, ALL SERVICE WAS DECLINED in favor of preserving the integrity and originality of this coveted timepiece for buyer. All paperwork from Audemars Piguet to be provided with sale.

DESCRIPTION:

Scarce and elegant Audemars Piguet Canape Automatic Rectangular Watch with Swivel Lugs in 18K solid yellow gold. Complete model reference is 15091BA.0.0002CR.01. Audemars Piguet Canape is unique amongst modern AP timepieces that it is designed with swivel lugs and still housing an automatic movement. The Canape along with Jules Audemars and Edward Piguet are modern models meant to pay homage to vintage Art Deco designs. The AP Canape’s swivel lugs, the domed crystal, and the narrow rectangular profile all harkon back to the Art Deco era. However, the dial is a highly modern touch as well as is the automatic movement caliber 2140 with 31 jewels. Obviously with a modern watch like the Canape the size, water resistance, deployant folding buckle and more are superior to vintage watches in terms of functionality. Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) $30,900.

Iconic Offshore. Timeless Design.
The 26020ST Black Themes is one of the earliest and most collectible iterations of the Royal Oak Offshore, carrying the signature Méga Tapisserie dial and a rugged, masculine aesthetic. Introduced in the early 2000s, this reference marks a key chapter in AP’s history, blending traditional Swiss craftsmanship with bold, modern sportiness. Housed in a 42mm stainless steel case and powered by the AP caliber 2326/2840, it features a robust automatic chronograph with a striking black dial and subdials. The watch wears comfortably on a black leather strap and comes complete with the original wooden box, hangtag, and papers. As one of the original "Themes" models, its place in Offshore collecting is well-established and continues to grow in desirability. With its iconic case shape and detailed finishing, this is a serious piece for anyone collecting important AP references.

A must-have for Royal Oak Offshore collectors and enthusiasts looking for a timeless and highly wearable AP chrono with provenance and growing collector value.

Pricing

Our prices reflect the competitive value of new and unworn watches, complete with all accessories and a valid manufacturer’s warranty. While we do accept offers to close deals, please understand that our prices are already highly competitive in the market.

Inspection

Before shipping, every watch undergoes a thorough inspection to ensure it matches the description in the listing. We check the timekeeping, case, bracelet, and overall condition, ensuring it’s in perfect order. Each watch is securely packed in its box to ensure safe delivery.

Returns

Buyers have two weeks to inspect their purchase. If the watch is not as described or does not function properly, you may return it for a full refund. Returns will only be accepted if the watch is in the same condition as it was shipped, including all original paperwork and accessories. Watches that have been worn, used, or altered in any way will not be eligible for a return.

Taxes

Sales tax is calculated and added at checkout based on your shipping address, in accordance with state and local laws. Chrono24 collects and remits these taxes directly, and we do not have the ability to waive or alter this amount.

Shipping & Delivery

• United States: All packages are shipped via Overnight shipping and can be delivered to a business address, FedEX office (Hold For Pickup) or a residential address.

• International: We welcome international buyers, and duties/customs fees are the buyer’s responsibility. Certain countries may have shipping restrictions due to insurance limitations, so please contact us to confirm availability.

Why Choose Us?

• Over 24 years of expertise and countless satisfied customers worldwide.
• A commitment to authenticity, transparency, and customer satisfaction.
• Competitive pricing, a 2-year warranty, and secure worldwide shipping.
• Chrono24’s escrow system ensures your funds are secure until your watch is delivered and confirmed.

We look forward to making your next watch purchase seamless and enjoyable. If you have any questions or need assistance, feel free to reach out for a consultation

PLEASE READ OUR GUIDELINES BELOW!

For safety reasons and to minimize risk of shipping loss, we must ship to Fedex Office or Ship Centers, where you can pick up your watch at your convenience. Please use the nearest Fedex Office or Ship Center as your shipping address on your order; this will prevent further delays for address changes. We will ensure the address is correct prior to confirming your order; do not worry.

Feel free to message us with any questions! If there is no Fedex Center near you, please use your home address as your shipping address and we will ensure we find an appropriate solution. Residential deliveries are still possible.

ROLEX Watches: All Rolex watches sold by us come with the Original Box, Green Chronometer Tag, booklets, and Warranty Card (if stated that “papers” are included in the listing information). The Warranty Card will be named and dated if applicable. Stickers will have been removed at the time of purchase from the Authorized Rolex Dealer, unless otherwise stated. The watch may be sized from the time of purchase however it has never been worn (if condition stated as “Unworn/New”) and comes with all standard links that come on Rolex Bracelet models. It may also come with the white serial tag, but that is not guaranteed as some Authorized Dealers keep the tag. Because we often stock several of the same model watch, even if the watch is pictured with the white serial tag, it is not guaranteed to come with it. The watch may also be newer than the noted on our listing. If we list as a 2021 Unworn, you may receive a 2022 Unworn. It is possible that the pictured watch in the ad has “stickers” on the watch, but you will receive a watch with stickers removed unless otherwise stated in the listing. You may receive a watch that has been wrapped in plastic by us, to prevent scratches during transit. Bezel protectors, even if pictured, are not always included.

Shipping

We prefer to use the Fedex Hold-At-Location service, meaning we ship your watch to the Fedex Office or Ship Center nearest you, where you can pick it up upon arrival. All of our shipments are insured and shipped Priority Overnight.

International shipping to select countries is done via Fedex International Priority shipping. Please be aware of all appropriate customs and import taxes you may incur. Failure to accept an international package will result in restocking fees on a case-by-case basis. We will ship to the nearest Fedex Ship Center to you, and you can pickup the package at your convenience.

In Person Showings

For safety reasons, we do not encourage in person showings or browsing. All of our operations are online and we deliver via Fedex Priority Overnight.

Trades

We happily take trades of Rolex, Omega, Audemars Piguet, Patek Philippe and most major brands towards any of our listed watches. If you wish to trade in your watch, please send photos and relevant information in your initial message, along with your desired trade value. Our offer for your watch is not binding and is subject to change or revocation. We process all of our trades via insured Fedex Priority Overnight shipping; we ship the watch you are receiving after we receive your watch and the appropriate cleared payment, if a balance is due to us.

Price Suggestions

We may have Price Suggestions enabled on some of our listings; this does not mean we are obligated to negotiate on these listings nor provide price reductions.

Payment

Payment is accepted through Chrono24 Trusted Checkout. We cannot hold watches for longer than 24 hours without proof of payment / payment confirmation. Please submit payment for your watch as soon as your order is confirmed to avoid delays and order cancellation.

All purchases are subject to the sales tax rate of your shipping address.

Returns

We offer a 100% Money Back Guarantee on all of our watches! Feel free to shop with confidence. You have 14 days to return your item. Watches must be in the exact same condition as they were shipped, not sized or altered in any way. If watches are not returned in the same condition, restocking fees may apply.

*We are not an authorized dealer for any of the brands that we list (as are all resellers), however all of our watches are guaranteed authentic.*

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore Havana is a bold yet refined interpretation of the Offshore chronograph, instantly recognizable for its warm tones and powerful wrist presence. Crafted in stainless steel, the 42mm case features the signature octagonal bezel with exposed screws, framing a rich brown “Méga Tapisserie” dial. Silver-toned subdials and luminous markers ensure excellent legibility, balancing sportiness with elegance.

Powered by the in-house Caliber 3126/3840 automatic chronograph movement, the watch delivers a 50-hour power reserve and can be admired through the sapphire exhibition caseback. It is paired with a brown alligator strap and comes with an additional blue rubber strap for versatile styling.

This example remains in very good condition, showing only light signs of wear. It is a complete full set, including the original box, papers, and accessories, making it a highly desirable addition to any serious collection.

The highly coveted Audemars Piguet Royal Oak 15500ST in 41mm stainless steel with a striking grey grand tapisserie dial, a modern icon that blends bold presence with refined detail. This reference captures everything that makes the Royal Oak legendary: the brushed stainless steel case and integrated bracelet, the octagonal bezel with exposed screws, and the exquisite textured dial that plays beautifully with light. Powered by the in-house Caliber 4302, it delivers a robust 70 hour power reserve, smooth sweep seconds, and exceptional finishing visible through the sapphire caseback. The timepiece is presented in mint condition and comes as a full set.

This impressive chronograph is crafted from lightweight titanium, which enhances comfort and durability. A striking blue Méga Tapisserie dial is featured, creating a vibrant and modern presence. A 43mm titanium case is used, offering a powerful yet refined profile. Moreover, a sapphire crystal caseback is included, allowing the movement to be admired. The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore design lineage is honored through the iconic octagonal bezel. Rubber pushers are fitted for a sporty and ergonomic feel. Additionally, a blue rubber strap is provided, giving the piece a cohesive and contemporary look.

Historical significance is felt through the Offshore’s legacy, which reshaped luxury sports watches in 1993. This model continues that bold tradition. The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore remains admired for innovation, craftsmanship, and unmistakable character. Introduced circa 2005, the Audemars Piguet Jules Audemars ref. 15056BC/O/0001CR/02 is a refined testament to classical watchmaking elegance. Housed in a slender 36 mm 18K white gold case with a sapphire display back, this dress watch reveals the beautifully finished Calibre 3090 manual‑wind movement, a 21‑jewel workhorse offering approximately 48 hours of power reserve. The silvered engine‑turned dial, adorned with applied Roman numerals and baton indices, is complemented by leaf‑shaped white gold hands, imparting a timeless and balanced aesthetic. With a modest 20 m water resistance, it remains a delicate yet practical daily companion. Notably, this reference is not a limited edition, yet its understated rarity and classic proportions make it a highly sought‑after piece among connoisseurs of elegant horology. The movement’s fine finishing and the case’s polished and satin surfaces reflect Audemars Piguet’s dedication to artisanal detail, while the overall design pays homage to the brand’s heritage in traditional round watchmaking. The watch’s thin profile and manual winding charm appeal to purists who value mechanical intimacy and historical lineage. Like New with Audemars Piguet box and undated papers.

The exquisite Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore "Half Brick" Rose Gold Ref. 26470OR.OO.A099CR.01 is here. This sumptuous timepiece showcases AP's mastery of precious metal craftsmanship with its stunning 18k rose gold construction paired with the rich chocolate brown dial that inspired its coveted nickname.

The iconic 42mm rose gold case displays the signature Royal Oak Offshore design language—octagonal bezel with exposed screws and bold proportions—while the warm precious metal adds an unmistakable luxury presence on the wrist. The deep chocolate "Méga Tapisserie" dial creates mesmerizing depth and texture, complemented perfectly by matching brown chronograph subdials and rose gold applied hour markers.

The distinctive "Half Brick" moniker comes from the substantial weight and presence this precious metal configuration delivers—a testament to the solid gold construction that sets this piece apart from steel variants. The interplay between the warm rose gold case and rich brown dial creates a harmonious color palette that exudes both sophistication and contemporary appeal.

Powered by AP's exceptional self-winding caliber 3126/3840 chronograph movement visible through the sapphire exhibition caseback, this timepiece represents the pinnacle of Swiss chronograph engineering wrapped in one of horology's most desirable precious metal configurations.

A true statement piece that combines Royal Oak Offshore's bold design heritage with the timeless allure of rose gold—perfect for the collector seeking both exclusivity and aesthetics.

This Audemars Piguet ultra-slim dress watch comes from a period when the brand was producing some of the most refined time-only wristwatches in Swiss watchmaking. During the 1950s, Audemars Piguet was deeply invested in thin manual-wind movements and restrained case design, supplying pieces that were intended for understated, daily elegance rather than technical bravado. These watches were made in relatively small numbers and were never mass-market products.

The additional Türler signature on the dial adds an important layer of historical context. Türler was one of the most respected Swiss retailers of the 20th century, and dual-signed dials were reserved for select clients and top-tier manufacturers. Seeing Türler paired with Audemars Piguet places this watch firmly in the upper tier of mid-century dress watches, both in quality and original retail positioning.

The white gold case is especially notable here. Unlike yellow gold examples that often show heavier polishing over decades, this case retains sharp geometry and honest surface wear, suggesting it has not been aggressively refinished. Combined with the minimalist dial layout, slender applied markers, and balanced proportions, this watch represents Audemars Piguet at its most classical and disciplined.

This is one of those watches that rewards close inspection. At a glance it feels simple, but the more time you spend with it, the more you appreciate the restraint. The unpolished white gold case has a quiet presence that you don’t see often, and the Türler signature gives it a bit of private-club character. It wears effortlessly, disappears under a cuff, and feels exactly like what a great dress watch from this era should be.

Every watch is thoroughly vetted, carefully described with complete transparency, and guaranteed authentic. Each piece is offered in honest condition, ensuring collectors receive a timepiece that reflects both originality and integrity.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak 26420CE — Overview

Reference: 26420CE.OO.A043VE
Collection: Royal Oak Selfwinding
Material: Full black ceramic
Case Size: 37 mm
Dial: Blue “Grande Tapisserie” with gradient/degradé effect
Movement: Automatic (self-winding)
Functions: Hours, minutes, central seconds, date

The 26420CE is a contemporary, bold interpretation of the Royal Oak sports watch. Its signature lies in combining cutting-edge material technology (ceramic) with Audemars Piguet’s classic Royal Oak design language.

Case & Bracelet

Material: Full black ceramic — case, bezel, and bracelet

Diameter: 37 mm — modern but wearable on a wide range of wrists

Bezel: Octagonal with eight polished screws — classic Royal Oak signature

Finish: Expert satin brushing with polished accents — ceramic finishing is extremely labor-intensive and produces a deep, matte-rich surface

Bracelet: Integrated ceramic bracelet with folding clasp

Black ceramic is prized for its scratch resistance, lightweight feel compared to metal, and modern aesthetic. It doesn’t develop patina like metals, and it keeps its finish over time.

Dial

Color: Blue, often with a gradient or “dégradé” effect — darker on the outer edges and lighter toward the center

Pattern: “Grande Tapisserie” — raised square texture unique to Royal Oak

Indices & Hands: Applied white gold or rhodium-toned markers and hands with luminous fill

Date Window: Positioned at 3 o’clock

The textured dial provides depth and contrast against the black ceramic, making the watch highly legible and visually dynamic.

Movement

Type: Automatic (self-winding)

Caliber: One of AP’s in-house selfwinding movements

Functions: Hours, minutes, central seconds, date

Power Reserve: Around 50–55 hours

Audemars Piguet movements are known for reliability, consistent performance, and quality finishing — even if not as decoration-heavy as some haute horlogerie pieces, they offer excellent practicality and durability.

Design & Appeal

The Royal Oak 26420CE stands out because it:

Uses full ceramic — rare in high-end integrated bracelet watches

Has a striking gradient blue “Grande Tapisserie” dial

Balances sportiness and elegance with the Royal Oak’s iconic shape

Is lightweight and comfortable due to ceramic construction

This model is ideal if you want a Royal Oak that feels modern and technical, yet still carries the design DNA of the original Gerald Genta Royal Oak.

Wearability

Size: 37 mm is versatile — not oversized, yet substantial because of the integrated bracelet

Weight: Ceramic keeps it light and comfortable

Aesthetic: Strong contrast between dial and case gives it presence on the wrist

The integrated bracelet design ensures that the watch sits securely and proportionately.

Summary

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak 26420CE is:

A 37 mm full black ceramic Royal Oak Selfwinding

Featuring a blue “Grande Tapisserie” gradient dial

With hours, minutes, seconds, and date functions

Powered by an automatic movement with ~50+ hours power reserve

Its mix of modern materials, iconic design, and refined mechanical engineering makes it one of the standout contemporary Royal Oak models — especially for collectors and enthusiasts who want something distinctive beyond traditional steel or gold references.==

A Bold Icon From the Offshore Lineup The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore 26170ST "Navy Panda" stands out with its crisp white “Méga Tapisserie” dial, perfectly contrasted by deep navy blue subdials and accents—a fan-favorite among collectors. Its 42mm stainless steel case and signature hexagonal bezel bring instant recognition, while the blue rubber strap enhances the sporty-luxury appeal. Powered by the AP Caliber 3126/3840 automatic chronograph movement, this reference delivers reliability and a 50-hour power reserve. The "Navy Panda" is particularly desirable due to its short production window and unique color combination, no longer available in current Offshore offerings. As part of the original generation of Royal Oak Offshore chronographs, it retains the classic bulk and charm that newer models have refined away. It’s a substantial wrist presence with balanced vintage-modern aesthetics. Whether worn daily or collected as an appreciating asset, this reference bridges bold style and mechanical prestige.

A discontinued classic with collector demand, this “Navy Panda” offers standout aesthetics, rock-solid movement performance, and investment appeal as one of the most recognizable Offshore references.

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Offshore reference 25940OK.OO.D002CA.01.A is a commanding 42 mm chronograph introduced in the mid‑2000s, here circa 2006, that exemplifies the brand’s pioneering “Rubber Clad” aesthetic. Its robust 18 k rose‑gold case is paired with a rubber‑coated bezel, crown, and pushers, creating a striking contrast against the grey “Méga Tapisserie” dial adorned with luminous Arabic numerals and rose‑gold hands. Powered by the in‑house automatic Caliber 2326/2840, this movement delivers approximately 40 hours of power reserve and chronograph precision, underscoring AP’s technical pedigree. With 100 m water resistance, the piece balances sportiness with high‑end finishing, while the integrated black rubber strap with rose‑gold folding clasp reinforces its bold, cohesive design. As a discontinued model, the Rubber Clad Offshore has become highly coveted among collectors for its unique material innovation and limited production run. This example’s combination of rose‑gold warmth, industrial rubber elements, and refined chronograph functionality makes it a standout in the Offshore lineage, offering both horological substance and aesthetic drama. Like New with Audemars Piguet box and papers dated 2011.

Audemars Piguet Royal Oak 26420CE.OO.A043VE — Overview

Reference: 26420CE.OO.A043VE
Collection: Royal Oak Selfwinding
Case Material: Black ceramic
Case Size: 37 mm
Bracelet: Black ceramic
Dial: Gradient blue (“Dégradé” effect)
Movement: Automatic
Style: Luxury sports watch with contemporary materials

This reference is part of Audemars Piguet’s modern Royal Oak lineup and is notable for its full ceramic case and bracelet combined with a gradient blue dial — a bold, contemporary combination that emphasizes both modern material technology and aesthetic drama.

Case & Bracelet

Material: Full black ceramic (case, bezel, and integrated bracelet)

Diameter: 37 mm — a contemporary yet substantial size that wears well on a variety of wrists

Bezel: Octagonal Royal Oak bezel with eight signature screws, also in ceramic

Finish: Satin-brushed surfaces with polished chamfers — ceramic finishing requires specialized techniques but delivers deep visual richness

Bracelet: Integrated ceramic links with folding clasp

Black ceramic offers outstanding scratch resistance, a lightweight feel compared to metal, and a bold, stealthy aesthetic not achievable with traditional materials.

Dial

Color: Blue gradient (darker at the edges, lighter toward the center)

Pattern: “Grande Tapisserie” with a Dégradé effect — adds visual depth and a sense of motion

Indices & Hands: Applied white gold or rhodium-toned hour markers and hands with luminous fill

Date Window: At 3 o’clock, integrated cleanly into the dial

The gradient blue dial creates dynamic color shifts depending on light, making the watch visually striking while still readable and refined.

Movement

Type: Automatic (self-winding)

Caliber: AP in-house self-winding movement

Functions: Hours, minutes, central seconds, date

Power Reserve: Approx. 50–55 hours

It delivers reliable performance and precision typical of Audemars Piguet’s standards for their selfwinding models.

Design & Appeal

This reference stands out within the Royal Oak range for its:

Full ceramic construction, which gives it a modern, technical look and feel

Gradient blue “Grande Tapisserie” dial, providing depth and color play

Integrated bracelet, a defining Royal Oak design element

The combination of high-tech ceramic and a vibrant dial positions this watch as a contemporary interpretation of the classic Royal Oak — less traditional than steel or gold versions, but striking in its aesthetic.

Wearability

Size: 37 mm balances presence without overwhelming the wrist

Weight: Ceramic makes it lighter than comparable metal references

Comfort: Integrated ceramic bracelet is comfortable and stays cool on the skin

Versatility: Great for both smart casual and dressier occasions

Ceramic also resists scratches better than steel and holds its finish extremely well over time.

Summary

The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak 26420CE.OO.A043VE is:

A 37 mm Royal Oak Selfwinding

Made from full black ceramic (case, bezel, and bracelet)

Featuring a gradient blue “Grande Tapisserie” dial

Powered by a reliable automatic movement

It expresses a bold, modern take on the Royal Oak concept — blending advanced materials, striking aesthetics, and the refined mechanics expected from Audemars Piguet.
